comment: Use a bleed build with Rivers of Blood katana, it melts the Elden Beast health quickly.
Always clean the Erdtree golden sigils off the ground before attacking or you take huge damage.
Stock up on Crimson Tears flasks and upgrade them to +12 before attempting this fight.
The Elden Beast swims away constantly. Use spirit ashes to close the distance faster.
Faith builds wreck this fight. Use Black Flame incantations, they deal percentage-based damage.
Target the glowing golden weak spots on its body for extra damage windows.
Use Mimic Tear ashes for Radagon phase, then Black Knife Tiche for Elden Beast, she avoids AOEs.
Do not stand too close during the constellation attack, it tracks you. Sprint sideways.
Bloodhound Step ash of war completely breaks this fight, use it to dodge the sword slam.
If you are struggling, summon the Mimic Tear. It doubles your DPS and splits boss attention.
Learn the golden sword slam combo: dodge into the boss, not away. Counter-intuitive but it works.
Rivers of Blood +10 with Lord of Bloods Exultation talisman is the meta pick for this boss.
Rogiers Rapture sorcery deals insane damage to the Elden Beast if you are a magic build.
Godfrey Icon talisman boosts charged spells, great for landing big hits between Elden Beast attacks.
Spam Blasphemous Blade skill, it heals on kill and the AoE hits the boss consistently.
For melee builds: Giant Hunt ash of war launches you into the air and deals great poise damage.
Bring at least 10 flasks going into Radagon and Elden Beast, it is a long back-to-back fight.
The golden rings it spawns on the ground will explode, roll through them, do not try to run away.
Grease your weapon with Holy-countering consumables if you are using a physical build.
Use the Cerulean Hidden Tear in your Wondrous Flask to cast spells for free during Elden Beast.
Vykes War Spear with bleed and madness is actually decent against Elden Beast, underrated pick.
Radagon is weak to fire. Bring Fire Grease or fire spells for phase 1 to save flask charges.
Your Physick flask combo should be Crimsonburst Crystal Tear and Opaline Hardtear for survivability.
Elden Beast has Holy resistance. Do not use Holy weapons here, go Physical or Fire instead.
Keep spacing awareness. The Elden Beast tail swipe comes out fast when you are underneath it.
You can interrupt some Elden Beast spellcasting with a heavy weapon stagger if you are quick.
Equip Erdtrees Favor talisman for extra HP and Stamina, this is a stamina-heavy fight.
Try using Swarm of Flies incantation to stack bleed from range when the boss swims away.
Power stance two Reduvia daggers and you can proc bleed every 2 to 3 hits on the Elden Beast.
Get your Vigor to at least 40 before this fight. One-shots are common if you are under 35.
If you die to Radagon, try memorizing his combos first before worrying about Elden Beast.
Elden Beast holy beam follows you, sprint horizontally the entire time to dodge it.
Comet Azur with Terra Magica setup deals absurd burst, you can one-phase Elden Beast sometimes.
Night Comet sorcery is great here since it is hard for the AI to dodge, cast during openings.
Dodge the Elden Stars meteor shower by sprinting diagonally, never in a straight line.
The Elden Beast gets more aggressive below 50 percent HP. Save a golden vow incantation for that.
Turtle Talisman extends stamina recovery. Very useful when dodging the Elden Beast constantly.
Do not fight near the water edges, the camera can get messed up and cause mistimed rolls.
If you are below level 130, go grind Farum Azula for runes before attempting this fight again.
Elden Beast weakness window is right after its big slam attacks, punish those hard.
The Elden Stars projectiles can be dodged by rolling toward where they came from, not away.
Bring Preserving Boluses if you accidentally proc Scarlet Rot from environmental hazards.
Do not rush the Radagon phase, play safe, conserve flasks, Elden Beast is the harder fight.
Dexterity builds: use Moonveil with its transient moonlight ash of war, incredible DPS.
You can cheese Elden Beast by staying directly under its belly, most attacks miss you there.
Ancestral Spirits Horn talisman restores FP on kill, great for spell users in this long fight.
Rykards Great Rune helps sustain HP during long boss fights if you have it equipped.
Bloodflame Blade incantation on any physical weapon adds bleed buildup, solid on melee builds.
Try jumping attacks during the Elden Beast recovery animations for poise break potential.
If you use spirit ashes, make sure they are at +10, they survive much longer in this fight.
Bloodhound Step is mandatory for dodging Waterfowl Dance. Do not try to outrun it.
Malenia heals on every hit, even if you block. Two-hand your weapon and learn to dodge.
Frost and bleed combo destroys Malenia. Use dual Cold Uchigatanas with Seppuku.
In phase 2, Malenia gains a Scarlet Rot dive bomb. Sprint away the moment she flies up.
Mimic Tear +10 is the best summon for Malenia. It splits aggro and matches your build.
Stagger her with jump attacks. She has surprisingly low poise for how aggressive she is.
Black Knife Tiche ash is incredible here. Tiche dodges Waterfowl and deals percentage HP damage.
Bring Preserving Boluses for phase 2 Scarlet Rot. She applies it with the butterfly attacks.
Malenia can be stun-locked with dual Colossal weapons if you time your jump attacks right.
Flame of the Redmanes ash of war staggers her in 2-3 hits. Absolutely broken against her.
Phase 1 tip: stay at mid range. She whiffs most combos and you get free running R2 punishes.
Rivers of Blood works but she dodges the initial slash sometimes. Use it during her recovery.
Waterfowl Dance has 3 flurries. First: run away. Second: dodge into her. Third: dodge again.
She is weak to frost. Hoarfrost Stomp plus a bleed weapon means double status procs.
Do NOT use greatshields. Her lifesteal works on blocked hits, you will never outdamage the healing.
For a first playthrough, go Vagabond class and pump Vigor to 40 before anything else.
Quality build (STR/DEX even) lets you try every weapon. Most beginner-friendly approach.
Bleed builds are overpowered. Get Arcane to 45 and use Rivers of Blood or Mohgs Spear.
Pure strength with a Greatsword or Grafted Blade is simple and effective for new players.
Intelligence builds struggle early but become god-tier with Comet Azur and Moonveil late game.
Faith builds are versatile. You get heals, damage spells, and buffs all in one build.
Do not neglect Endurance. You need stamina for dodging and heavy armor. 25 minimum.
Brass Shield from the soldiers at Gatefront is the best medium shield you will find for hours.
Prisoner class gives you Intelligence plus Dex for a spellblade build. Very fun and strong.
The Uchigatana from the Deathtouched Catacombs is free and carries you through most of the game.
Confessor class is the best starting class overall. Good stats, has heal, starts with a shield.
Level Mind to at least 20 if you plan on using any magic or ashes of war regularly.
Golden Halberd from the Tree Sentinel is the strongest early weapon if you can beat him.
Dual wielding two of the same weapon type gives you power stance attacks. Massive DPS increase.
Radagons Soreseal gives you 20 free levels of stats. Best talisman in the game for the first half.
